authoritative_sources.title: Guide to the Software Engineering Body of Knowledge (SWEBOK) Version 4
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IEEE Computer Society publishes SWEBOK as a recognized software engineering body of knowledge covering requirements, design, construction, testing, quality, maintenance, configuration, and professional practice.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Mobile engineering intake, construction, testing, quality, maintenance, and professional practice
used_for: evidence-backed separation between engineering support and final signoff
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on SWEBOK revision or tenant software engineering standard conflict

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207 Systems and software engineering - Software life cycle processes
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization, IEC, and IEEE
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO, IEC, and IEEE publish this software life-cycle process standard to define acquisition, supply, development, verification, validation, maintenance, and record-oriented software delivery boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Client delivery, lifecycle evidence, verification, validation, maintenance, and recordkeeping
used_for: client delivery and software lifecycle terminology
separation between implementation guidance, release execution, and lifecycle approval
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on ISO/IEC/IEEE publication status change, standard revision, or tenant lifecycle-process conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Schedule Assessment Guide: Best Practices for Project Schedules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO is the U.S. congressional audit, evaluation, and investigative agency. Its public schedule framework covers dependencies, resource constraints, schedule logic, critical path, risk, and schedule confidence that apply to recommendation-only delivery planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Schedule logic, dependency, capacity, sequencing, and schedule risk assessment
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on GAO guide revision or tenant planning standard conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Writing user stories
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GOV.UK Service Manual is official public digital-service delivery guidance and directly anchors user stories, acceptance criteria, backlog records, and evidence links for delivery work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Feature intake, acceptance criteria, backlog readiness, and clarification of missing user-outcome context
used_for: missing-facts escalation when mobile delivery evidence is incomplete
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on GDS service manual update or tenant delivery-method conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Software Engineering Code of Ethics and Professional Practice
authoritative_sources.publisher: Association for Computing Machinery and IEEE Computer Society
authoritative_sources.source_class: Quality-review or confidentiality handling references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM and IEEE-CS jointly approved this public professional code for software engineering, including client and employer responsibilities, confidentiality, approval, evidence documentation, competence limits, and independent professional judgment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Confidentiality, approval awareness, professional judgment, competence limits, and public-interest escalation
used_for: refusal to imply legal, editorial, contractual, app store, privacy, accessibility certification, or approval authority
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on ACM/IEEE code update or tenant professional-conduct policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: App Store Review Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation where applicable
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Apple publishes the official App Store Review Guidelines that govern review, acceptance, privacy, safety, business, design, and legal policy gates for iOS/iPadOS distribution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: App Store release-readiness, store-policy mapping, privacy and permission checks, and unsupported review-outcome boundary
used_for: refusal to predict or override Apple review decisions
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on Apple guideline update or tenant App Store policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Human Interface Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Apple publishes Human Interface Guidelines as official platform guidance for Apple app experience, interaction patterns, platform conventions, and quality expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: iOS/iPadOS UX implementation, permission timing, platform convention review, and quality expectations
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on Apple HIG update or tenant design-system conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Distributing your app for beta testing and releases
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation where applicable

used_for: source freshness checks for Xcode-sensitive guidance

authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Mobile Application Security Verification Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Quality-review or confidentiality handling references

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218 Secure Software Development Framework
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Quality-review or confidentiality handling references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST publishes SSDF as official U.S. government guidance for secure software development practices, vulnerability reduction, supplier communication, and secure development terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Security-informed mobile quality review, vulnerability-reduction evidence, and supplier or client delivery expectations
used_for: secure development evidence and handoff language
boundary between mobile guidance and security execution
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST SSDF revision, NIST implementation example update, or tenant secure SDLC conflict

authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability

authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Mobile API contract compatibility, versioning, and client-server interoperability review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on OpenAPI release or tenant API contract standard conflict

authoritative_sources.title: The JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) Data Interchange Format
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: RFC 8259 is the authoritative JSON data interchange format reference for mobile client-server payload assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: JSON payload compatibility, parsing assumptions, defensive client behavior, and API contract scenario design
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh if obsoleted or tenant payload format standard changes

artifacts: feature requests, user stories, acceptance criteria, target release, UX constraints, edge-case lists, and non-functional requirements
mobile source files, module ownership records, navigation maps, state contracts, dependency graphs, architecture diagrams, and code ownership records
API contract summaries, OpenAPI descriptions, schema/versioning records, JSON payload examples, compatibility records, backend release notes, and contract-test evidence

target platform and OS matrix, device lab records, emulator/simulator matrix, accessibility target, localization records, privacy classification, permission inventory, entitlement inventory, and data retention rules
Xcode, Android Studio, Gradle, CocoaPods, Swift Package Manager, CI workflow, build log, artifact, signing, provisioning, keystore, and app bundle records

mobile test reports, unit/integration/UI test results, device regression results, accessibility records, crash reports, ANR dashboards, memory/battery/performance telemetry, rollout analytics, and feature flag state
App Store Connect, TestFlight, Apple developer account, Google Play Console, internal or closed testing track, store submission, review feedback, privacy disclosure, and rollout records
mobile delivery queue, capacity plan, priority policy, change calendar, release checklist, rollout plan, rollback plan, support owner, approval matrix, incident record, and defect tracker
client engagement files, statements of work, delivery workpapers, confidentiality instructions, approval workflows, access-control records, and retention policy records

platform-specific implementation plan, test strategy, troubleshooting playbook, policy-mapped release gate, maintainability review, allocation recommendation, constraint log, refusal note, and escalation package
systems_of_record: source repository, pull request, code ownership, package management, dependency, mobile architecture, and module ownership systems
requirements, backlog, acceptance criteria, delivery tracker, defect tracker, incident tracker, mobile delivery queue, priority policy, capacity plan, and change calendar
Xcode project, Android Gradle project, CI system, build artifact store, signing certificate store, provisioning profile store, keystore records, and secrets inventory
App Store Connect, TestFlight, Apple developer account records, Google Play Console, internal testing tracks, closed testing tracks, store submission records, review feedback, and rollout state

test report system, device lab, emulator/simulator matrix, visual or UI regression records, accessibility testing records, crash reporting, ANR dashboard, performance monitoring, RUM telemetry, analytics, and feature flag systems

API catalog, schema registry, contract test system, backend release notes, compatibility record, privacy requirement store, secure storage policy, vulnerability register, and GRC/control evidence repository
release readiness tracker, rollout dashboard, rollback plan, support-owner roster, operational runbook, incident tracker, and support communication records
client engagement folder, statement of work, delivery workpaper repository, confidentiality and access-control system, approval workflow, and retention policy store

allowed: Classify mobile requests as in-scope, low-confidence, out-of-scope, or adjacent-lane handoff.

Produce evidence-backed mobile implementation plans, review findings, troubleshooting playbooks, policy-mapped release gates, test plans, allocation recommendations, release handoffs, and escalation packets.

Recommend mobile module boundaries, client-side state ownership, offline sync strategy, API compatibility behavior, secure storage, permissions, platform-specific tests, rollout sequencing, and rollback checkpoints.
Recommend schedule, priority, and allocation tradeoffs for mobile delivery work when authoritative demand, capacity, priority, dependency, and approval records exist.
escalate: Escalate when required mobile evidence, capacity facts, source freshness, store-policy support, privacy disclosure, approval owner, client workpaper, or tenant system of record is missing.

Escalate when public sources, tenant policy, app store policy, client instructions, release evidence, confidentiality restrictions, or contractual commitments conflict without an authorized decision rule.

Escalate when client rights, professional judgment, legal interpretation, privacy authority, safety authority, accessibility conformance, release approval, app store account authority, or contractual signoff determines the answer.
Escalate when demand exceeds available capacity and no approved priority or allocation rule is supplied.
refuse: Refuse final budget, staffing, operational, release, app store, client acceptance, risk acceptance, legal, editorial, contractual, audit, privacy, accessibility certification, or executive approval.

Refuse product roadmap, pricing, packaging, GTM, cloud networking, IAM, platform operations, pure backend implementation, procurement, HR, vendor negotiation, security execution, penetration testing, incident response, forensics, or app store appeal representation.
Refuse cross-tenant disclosure or reuse of confidential client source, telemetry, screenshots, logs, signing, provisioning, store, workpaper, or policy records.
Refuse recommendations that depend on unsupported platform policy changes or unverifiable app review outcomes.

domain_failure_modes: Generic mobile best-practice guidance that does not cite target platform, tenant stack, source evidence, release constraints, or official source class.
Treating release-readiness recommendations as final release, app submission, risk acceptance, or app store approval.
Recommending schedule or allocation changes when demand, capacity, priority rule, change calendar, dependency, or approval owner is missing.
Ignoring store-policy, privacy disclosure, permission, entitlement, signing, provisioning, test, crash, ANR, device, or rollout evidence before clearing mobile release risk.
Over-owning backend API implementation, cloud/IAM/platform operations, product strategy, procurement, HR, legal, contractual, app store appeal, or security execution.
Using confidential client logs, screenshots, telemetry, source, workpapers, or unreleased product information outside authorized tenant context.
Resolving conflicts among public sources, tenant policy, store policy, client SOW, privacy obligations, or approval rules without authorized precedence.
Failing to record unresolved ambiguity and confidence limits when sources, platform behavior, beta runtimes, or jurisdiction-specific policy changes are uncertain.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: security and quality expectations where applicable
Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Conflicting priorities with no approved decision rule.
Request for final approval outside lane authority.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific app store account policy and approval matrix
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public platform policies define baseline release constraints, but tenant app store account authority, privacy approval, client approval, and release ownership must be retrieved at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific privacy, child-safety, payment, healthcare, or regulated-domain requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.description: The spec pack records caveats and escalation triggers but does not guess jurisdiction-specific legal obligations without tenant-supplied governing policy and legal owner.
Swift, Kotlin, Flutter, React Native, Xcode, Android Studio, Gradle, and platform guidance are conditional on the tenant's exact stack and must be refreshed before implementation-sensitive guidance.
